exemplar.title: An investigation into the the impact of fertiliser pollution in the Baltic sea on a local algae specie, Tetraselmis suecicaâs growth

detailedExemplarData.title: An investigation into the the impact of fertiliser pollution in the Baltic sea on a local algae specie, Tetraselmis suecicaâs growth

subject.title: Biology
subject.description:
**IB Biology** is a **Group 4** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **four interconnected themes: Unity and Diversity, Form and Function, Interaction and Interdependence, and Continuity and Change**, with a strong focus on **experimental work, scientific inquiry, data analysis, and practical investigation skills**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2025**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| Assessment | Format | SL | HL | Weighting |
|------------|--------|----|----|-----------|
| **Paper 1** | Multiple-choice and data-based questions | 1 hour 20 min | 2 hours | 36% |
| **Paper 2** | Short and extended response questions | 1 hour 30 min | 2 hours 30 min | 44% |
| **Scientific Investigation** | Individual research project | 10 hours | 10 hours | 20% |

strengths: Research question is sharply focused, clearly stated twice and drives the entire investigation.
Methodology is comprehensive, with justified selection of techniques, proper controls and appropriate statistical tests.
Topic is communicated accurately throughout, with detailed background, contextual maps and mechanistic explanations.
weaknesses: Mechanistic discussion of GSâGOGAT and TCA links could be strengthened by citation of a recent review source.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: All three strands of Focus and Method clearly meet the descriptors for the top band. The question is explicitly focused and testable, the methods are fully justified and the topic is communicated accurately and effectively. The only minor lapse is the absence of one up-to-date citation for a biochemical mechanism.

strengths: Consistent and accurate use of discipline-specific terminology (e.g., NHââº, GSâGOGAT, Tukey HSD) throughout.
Source material is applied coherently to support rationale, statistical calculations and experimental choices.
weaknesses: Engagement with some sources remains descriptive rather than critically comparative.
Reliance on older taxonomy papers without critical appraisal limits depth of synthesis.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Terminology use clearly reaches the highest band, but application of source material is mostly effective rather than fully critical or synthesized. This mixed performance justifies a score of 5 in Knowledge and Understanding.

strengths: Data analysis is detailed: means, SDs, CVs, ANOVA and Tukey tests are calculated and linked explicitly to the research question.
Conclusions are generally well supported and limitations are acknowledged with proposed improvements.
Argument flows logically from biochemical mechanisms through data interpretation to broader environmental implications.
weaknesses: Some over-interpretation of overlapping error bars.
Limited discussion of operator variability and instrument calibration in assessing data reliability.
Occasional minor logical leaps when asserting the efficacy of target concentrations without full statistical support.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Analysis consistently relates results to the research question and employs appropriate statistical methods, placing it firmly in the 7â9 band. Evaluation and argumentation are strong but not sufficiently critical for the top band, so a score of 9 is warranted.

strengths: Structure follows the prescribed format with clear section headings, numbered figures and tables.
Consistent formatting, correct application of layout conventions and inclusion of word count, title and page numbers enhance readability.
weaknesses: Minor typographical errors (e.g., duplicated words in the title).
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The presentation meets the highest band descriptors for clear, appropriate structure and correctly applied layout conventions. Only small typographical glitches prevent absolute perfection.

strandSelections.reasoning: The essay follows the prescribed EE structure (title page, abstract omitted per new guide, contents page, introduction, methodology, results, discussion, conclusion, evaluation, bibliography, appendices). Section and sub-section headings aid navigation, and tables/figures are logically sequenced. Minor redundancy and occasional lengthy tables embedded in the text slightly impede flow, but overall the structure is clear and appropriate, fulfilling the top band for Presentation-Structure.

Formal requirements are largely met: consistent font, spacing and heading styles; table/figure captions and numbering; word count, title and page numbers are provided. Data tables and graphs are legible and units are shown. Minor issues (occasional formatting glitches, some oversized images, mixed bold/italic tags) do not detract from overall clarity. Hence the work satisfies the descriptors for correct application of layout conventions in the highest band for this strand.

A coherent line of argument links Baltic eutrophication, nitrogen loading and experimental outcomes. Claims are generally substantiated with data and literature, and the structure moves logically from introduction to conclusion. Nevertheless, moments of repetition, minor logical leaps (e.g., asserting target concentrationâs efficacy without statistical support) and some descriptive passages interrupt the otherwise clear reasoning. These minor inconsistencies align with the 4â6 band rather than the consistently integrated, sophisticated argumentation of the highest band.

The topicâfertiliser-derived nitrogen effects on the growth of Tetraselmis suecicaâis presented with a concise title, a clear statement of environmental context (Baltic eutrophication) and a well-developed biological rationale supported by literature, graphs and mechanistic pathways (e.g., GSâGOGAT and TCA links). Background information is wide-ranging and precise, enabling the reader to understand why the chosen concentrations and species are pertinent. Explanations are consistently accurate and embedded throughout the introduction, methods and discussion, demonstrating comprehensive communication of the topic.

A substantial range of peer-reviewed articles, governmental reports and technical manuals is cited, and key concepts (eutrophication, nitrogen assimilation, GS-GOGAT pathway) are integrated into the rationale and discussion. Source material is generally relevant and used to support claims, yet the engagement is somewhat descriptive; synthesis across sources is occasional and some literature (e.g., very old taxonomy paper) is used without critical appraisal. Thus application is coherent and mostly effective, fitting the 3â4 band.

The data are extensively processed: means, percentage changes, CVs, SDs and ANOVA/Tukey tests are calculated and tabulated. Graphs include error bars linked to appropriate uncertainty measures, and the student comments on their significance. The analysis consistently relates trends and statistical outcomes to the research question and to biochemical mechanisms. Some over-interpretation of overlapping error bars and limited exploration of confounding nutrients prevent a fully âexcellentâ (10â12) level, but the breadth and logical linkage of analysis place it securely in the 7â9 band.

The student designs a coherent experimental protocol: preliminary trials justify microscopic counting over spectrophotometry; five replicates per treatment plus a control are used; detailed tables show calculation of fertiliser doses from Baltic inlet data; control of salinity, light and temperature is addressed; ethical and safety considerations are articulated. Statistical tests (ANOVA + Tukey) are appropriate and assumptions are at least considered. While duration is short and contamination occurred, the chosen methods and source material are unquestionably relevant and selection decisions are explicitly justified, meriting the top band.

The discussion interprets results in light of nitrogenâs biochemical role and compares findings with multiple studies, showing awareness of broader implications for Baltic eutrophication policy. Limitations (duration, light variability, mixed nutrients) are acknowledged with feasible improvements. However, evaluation of data reliability (e.g., bacterial contamination, counting error) is somewhat brief and the conclusion occasionally overstates statistical certainty. Overall the evaluation is good, with supported conclusions, but lacks the depth and critical balance required for the top band.

The research question is explicitly stated twice (title page and section 1.1), is sharply focused on a single, quantifiable dependent variable (population growth rate over five days) and a narrowly defined independent variable (four nitrogen concentrations reflecting Baltic Sea data). The question is testable within the constraints of an EE and aligns directly with the investigationâs scope, data collection and analysis, showing full focus and relevance.

Throughout the essay the student consistently employs discipline-specific terminology (e.g., b{NH_4^+}, GS-GOGAT, coefficient of variation, Tukey HSD) accurately and in context. Concepts such as Poisson distribution, eutrophication mechanisms and algal metabolic pathways are explained correctly, with equations and units appropriately formatted. Occasional minor lapses (e.g., duplicated words) do not impede clarity. The precise and sustained use of biological and statistical vocabulary clearly meets the highest band.

annotations.comment: The cell-counting rules acknowledge random sampling bias but lack discussion of inter-operator variability or replicate consistency checks.
Procedure steps are logically ordered and detailed, ensuring reproducibility of solution preparation and sampling protocols.
Figure 1 effectively illustrates the eutrophication process, integrating visual and textual description to support the written rationale.
The investigationâs title clearly identifies the species, pollutant and context, providing a focused and informative introduction to the studyâs scope.
The word count is provided and falls within the 4 000-word limit, meeting the formatting requirement.
The title contains a duplicated word (âthe theâ), which detracts from presentation clarity; please correct this typographical error.
The research question is explicitly stated, focused on quantitative population growth over five days and directly guides the experimental design.
The table of contents is comprehensive and correctly ordered, facilitating easy navigation through sections and subsections.
Background information is largely descriptive; to strengthen critical engagement, explicitly compare differing source perspectives on eutrophication mechanisms.
Use of the Poisson-distribution formula (SD = ân) and CV formula demonstrates accurate application of statistical concepts to uncertainty analysis.
The description of preliminary trials and justification for selecting microscopic counting is well supported by comparative data, showing sound methodological choice.
The equipment list omits important calibrations and error ranges for key items (e.g., pipette calibration date), which could affect data reliability.
Calculation examples for deriving fertiliser masses from nitrogen concentration are clear and demonstrate accurate stoichiometric reasoning.
The calculation for stock concentration uses a shorthand equation that may confuse readers; expand and format the dilution equation more clearly.
The reliance on Norris et al. (1980) for life-cycle details would benefit from a recent source to confirm that the chosen strain exhibits identical life-history traits.
The caption âfigure 1â should be capitalized (âFigure 1â) to maintain consistency with formatting conventions.
The geographic and hydro-topographic context of the Baltic Sea is clearly explained, underlining its vulnerability to nutrient retention.
Figures 2 and 3 are presented without in-text analysis of differing pressure categories; briefly interpret how each pressure influences eutrophication trends.
The bathymetric map (Figure 4) is well integrated, providing spatial context for water exchange dynamics that impact nutrient accumulation.
The description of the dependent variable lists two uncertainty metrics (CV and SD) without fully explaining how each will be applied to error analysis; clarify this linkage.
The description of the GSâGOGAT and TCA links could be enhanced by citing a recent review to support the mechanistic claims with up-to-date evidence.
The biochemical pathway (Figure 6) and its textual explanation accurately employ discipline-specific terminology and reflect deep subject understanding.
The null and alternative hypotheses are clearly and formally stated using appropriate notation (Hâ, Hâ), demonstrating strong focus and method formulation.
Independent and dependent variables are precisely defined and justified with literature, ensuring methodological clarity and focus.
The controlled-variables table lacks a grammar check (â0 ml measuring cylinderâ is likely a typo); please confirm equipment details for accuracy.
Referencing Figure 7 before its caption disrupts reader flow; reposition or re-label to maintain logical sequence in presentation.
